turns.content: I'm having trouble with my CI/CD pipeline failing intermittently. I suspect it's related to network interfaces changing during builds, but I don't know how to reliably detect those changes in Rust.

That makes sense. One way is to use netlink sockets in Rust—specifically the rtnetlink crate—to monitor link events and routing table changes. That way you can react accordingly during your pipeline runs.
Oh, I thought I could just watch /sys/class/net for interface changes but that's not reliable enough. So using rtnetlink is the better approach?

Yes, you're on the right track! rtnetlink provides a more robust, kernel-level event-driven mechanism compared to polling `/sys`. It lets you subscribe to link events (up/down), address changes, and routing table updates. This is especially useful during build times when interfaces might be brought down or up.
